calendar.matters:
Notice Of New Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Adminstrative Mandamus [Ccp 1094.5]
Off Calendar May-28-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Wayne Snodgrass, Deputy City Attorney, 415.554.4700, for defendant City and County of San Francisco; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER REYNALDO VARGAS' Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus [CCP 1094.5] is denied. (CCP 1094.5.) First, the punitive action against Petitioner is not barred by the statute of limitations. (Gov. Code 3304(d)(1).) The administrative record does not support a finding that Petitioner's misconduct was "discovered by a person authorized to initiate an investigation" prior to October 8, 2010. Officers Hilder and Overand were not superior officers who could initiate an investigation. (Department General Order nos. 1.06(I)(A)(4), 3.02 ("DGO").) The audit of overtime and premium pay within the police department was issued by the Office of the City Controller. (Admin. Record, p. AR000417 - AR000466.) The audit provided "28 recommendations for the department to improve how it controls the use of overtime and premium pay." (Admin Record, p. AR000419.) It did not pertain to identifying individual violators. The filing of charges on September 28, 2009 was timely and did not violate Petitioner's due process rights. Second, the decision and punishment are supported by the weight of the evidence. Petitioner was charged with 14 incidences of violating DGO no. 11.01(B)(5)(c). The punishment was not excessive. (See Deegan v. City of Mountain View (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 37, 45.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Maria Viscio #12353.
